resources.string: Watches for the Instagram Reels tab and automatically backs out of it. Only reads on-screen content from Instagram -- does not read or store any personal data, and does not act in any other app.

1. Tap "Open Accessibility Settings" and enable "Reels Blocker Service". If the switch won't stay on: App info -> ⋮ menu (top right) -> "Allow restricted settings".

2. Tap "Open App Battery Settings" and set battery usage to "No restrictions" and enable Autostart (Xiaomi/HyperOS specific).

3. Same screen -> Other permissions -> allow "Display pop-up windows while running in the background".

4. Also open recent apps and tap the lock icon on Reels Blocker so HyperOS doesn't kill it in the background -- if the service ever shows as "not working" in Accessibility settings, this is usually why, and re-enabling the toggle fixes it.

5. Use Run/Stop above to pause blocking anytime, per app.

6. Tap the arrow top-left -> Log if something misbehaves -- copy it and send it over so it can be fixed precisely.
The log is stored locally on this device only -- nothing is sent anywhere. Copy it and share it if something breaks.
Accessibility service NOT enabled -- see Settings in the menu
%1$s detection isn\'t implemented yet -- Run/Stop is saved for when it is, but nothing is blocked or tracked for it yet.
Blocking Instagram Reels. Tap Stop in the app to pause.
